S below bear bar closing near 
1st bar of day has only 20% chance of being either H or L of day 
If big bull bar after gap down, then 30% chance of L of day 
If Big bear bar after ga1p up, then 30% chance of Hof day 
By close of bar 7, 50% chance day has already formed either H or L of day 
By close of bar 12, 70% chance day has already formed either H or L of day 
By close of bar 18, 80 - 90% chance day has already formed either Hor L of day 
By close of bar 80, 99.99% chance day has already formed either H or L of day

Easiest way is to reduce position size by taking some profits 
Wait for TR or bull channel before looking to sell

Your Data Is Sampled: CME Data Is Always Correct 
G@Brooks2018 
1st bar of day today with 
my broker's chart had 
open exactly at the low 
The low was correct but the 
open was 2 ticks too high 
[C!J] mr.nazari.ir 
The CME owns the Emini and it 
decides what the prices are 
Most brokers usually provide sampled data 
There are so many trades everyday, your computer 
could lock up if it showed every trade 
Your broker might miss an important price 
You can always confirm by going to 
www.CMEgroup.com 
Their data is delayed 10 minutes 
CME website shows there 
was actually a 2-tick tail 
at bottom of bar

Chart Data Is Often Sampled: Sometimes Not Exact 
G@Brooks2018 
~ 
Most brokers use sampled data and do not show every tick 
Here, the actual low of the day was below the low of the 1st bar, 
even though this chart shows the low to be at the open

Slide 19 

Emini Daily Chart 
All MTR bottoms 
are Double Bottoms 
Rarely perfect 
1. Minor reversal breaks above bear trend line 
2. PB in bull trend reaches EMA and 
has about 10 or more bars (fewer if climactic rally} 
3. Good if EMA Gap bar since sign of strong bulls 
{able to put bar completely above EMA) 
4. Resumption of selloff that tests the prior low 
5. Resumption down has about 10 bars 
and is not very strong 
6. Good buy signal bar 
2nd bottom is 3 ticks higher 
[C!J] mr.nazari.ir 
7. 40% chance of swing up 
8. Expect at least 2 legs up 
o 
ra 1ng curse.com

8ig Bar in TR: Limit Order Market 
C) @Brooks2018 
BO above big bull bar 
60% chance of higher prices 
But, not high enough probability! 
Do not B with stop above bar 
[C!J] mr.nazari.ir 
Big bar so big risk 
Late in day so only small reward 
Terrible risk/reward 
Even if win 60% of ti me, 
the losers would erase the winners 
Therefore odds favor limit order traders, 
selling above and buying below 
Wait for tomorrow

11- Final Bear Flag: ii Late in Bear Trend 
ii means Consecutive Inside Bars 
ii late in bear trend is often the Final Bear Flag 
Look for reversal up after 1- 3 bar bear BO 
Failed BO below Final Bear Flag 
B above bull bar closing near its H 
Better to wait for this 2nd B signal bar 
because the 1st was an ii in a 6 bar bear micro channel

[C!J] mr.nazari.ir 
When there is a lot of TR price action, 
Traders fade bad signal bars at the top and bottom 
Bears will sell above bear or weak buy signal bar 
in bull flag at top of TR 
Bulls will buy below bull or weak sell signal bar 
in bear flag at bottom of TR

70% chance of another reversal within 1st 10 minutes

before the announcement

FOMC 11 am PT 
FOMC 11 am PT 
Be ready for anything: 
Bull trend, bear trend, several reversals, tight TR, late BO up or down 
Usually get BO up and down within a minute of the report 
That means one of the two quickly fails 
60% chance of another reversal within 1st 10 minutes 
Wait for at least 10 minute for 
consecutive trend bars in one direction

,3 Day Bear Channel: Good Follow-Through after Big B()~,JliM 
• • • • • • 
• 
Channels usually have BO within 3 days 
Strong reversal up and BO above bear channel 
1st BO above TL (top of channel) so minor reversal 
TR more likely than bull trend 
2 days of Follow-Through Selling 
Bulls usually need test down to form Major Trend Reversal 
Major reversal means bull trend after bear trend 
Here, HL MTR but late in day

Gaps are rare on 5 min chart 
Most close before the end of the bar 
Most brokers provide only sampled data 
Gap down is rare on 5 min chart 
Usually closes before the bar closes 
Most gaps are caused by BAD DATA 
and not every tick, which could overload your computer 
Chart from CME 
shows no gap 
This chart shows a gap down, but CME data shows there was no gap 
However, enough computers see the gap for it to be a magnet 
E~
ct rally _tp close the gap by end of day 
Here, gap stayed open for 30+ bars, 
but closed by end of_ ay

Gap on 5 Min Chart: Sampling Error 
C) @Brooks2018 
No gap on 
chart on 
CME website 
mr.nazar1.1r 
Most charting services do not show every tick 
They provide sampled data 
It is close to perfect, but it sometimes misses important prices 
This is Sampling Error 
Here, the chart shows a gap 
But is you look at the CME website, there was no gap

· G~ap on 5 Min Chart: Usually Closes and Usually 8-ad [a1'iooks201s 
[C!J] mr.nazari.ir 
Most data vendors do not show every tick 
They have algorithms that sample the actual data from the CME 
Their computers would sometimes lock up if they processed every tick 
This sometimes results in gaps on the 5 min chart 
Most are caused by sampling error, like today 
Gap up on 5 min chart is 
usually caused by bad data --......-.. 
9 
Even if bad data and no actual gap on CM E's website, 
gap typically closes before end of day 
Chart from CME 
shows no gap

Buy Climax: 3 Types Are Low 1, Low 2, and Low 3 
G )@Brooks2018 
Low 1 (
11B Climax'') 
Big bull bar . 
or bars is 
Buy Climax 
Any Buy Climax 
can be after 
gap up or down 
Buy Climax that immediately 
reverses down is a Low 1 Top 
Just call it a ''Buy Climax'' bottom 
Low 2 (Double Top) 
1 
2 
+ 
Gap up so 
possible 
Ilf 
Buy Climax 
Buy Climax that needs 2 attempts to 
reverse down is a Low 2 Top 
But if the tops are about the same 
level and at about 10 or more bars 
apart, call it a Double Top 
This is a Low 2 top 
Low 3 (Wedge Top) 
Buy Climax that reverses down after 
3 legs up is a Wedge top

Every High 2 bottom is a DB 
Either term is always correct

a 2nd reversal up is a double bottom 
A High 2 Bottom is just a small DB 
(usually less than 10 bars)

~s Climax: 3 Types Are High 1, High 2, and High 3 
(Q:1 @Brooks2018 
High 1 (
11Sell Climax'') 
Any Sell Climax 
can be after 
gap up or down 
Sell Climax that immediately 
reverses up is a High 1 bottom 
Just call it a ''Sell Climax'' bottom 
High 2 (DB) 
1 
2 
Big bear 
bar is 
Sell Climax 
Sell Climax that needs 2 attempts 
to reverse up is a High 2 bottom 
If the bottoms are at about the 
same level, and about 10 or more 
bars apart, call it a Double Bottom 
This is a High 2 bottom 
'-" 
High 3 (Wedge Bottom) 
• • • • •• • • • • • •• • • • • • 
Sell Climax that reverses up after 
3 legs down is a Wedge bottom 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

Every High 2 bottom is a DB 
Either term is always correct

it is a High 2 Bottom

a 2nd reversal up is a double bottom 
A High 2 Bottom is just a small DB 
(usually less than 10 bars)

~e Climax: 3 Typ_es Are Low 1, Low 2, and Low 3 
G )@Brooks2018 
Low 1 (
11B Climax'') 
Big bull bar . 
or bars is 
Buy Climax 
Any Buy Climax 
can be after 
gap up or down 
Buy Climax that immediately 
reverses down is a Low 1 Top 
Just call it a ''Buy Climax'' bottom 
Low 2 (Double Top) 
1 
2 
+ 
Gap up so 
possible 
Ilf 
Buy Climax 
Buy Climax that needs 2 attempts to 
reverse down is a Low 2 Top 
But if the tops are about the same 
level and at about 10 or more bars 
apart, call it a Double Top 
This is a Low 2 top 
Low 3 (Wedge Top) 
Buy Climax that reverses down after 
3 legs up is a Wedge top

S Climax: 3 Types Are High 1,_ High 2, and High 3 
('-)@.Brooks2018 
~ 
High 1 (''Sell Climax'') 
Any Sell Climax 
can be after 
gap up or down 
Sell Climax that immediately 
reverses up is a High 1 bottom 
Just call it a ''Sell Climax'' bottom 
JO.Ji.Ji. .......... L.."'"' 
Ji. 
... 
High 2 (DB) 
1 
Big bear 
/ 
bar is 
£ ~ 
Sell Climax 
' 
1 
2 
Sell Climax that needs 2 attempts to 
reverse up is a High 2 bottom 
If the bottoms a re at a bout the 
same level and about 10 or more 
bars apart, call it a Double Bottom 
This is a High 2 bottom 
High 3 (Wedge Bottom) 
• • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • 
Sell climax that reverses up after 
3 legs down is a Wedge bottom

Bear Trend From The Open: Small PB Bear Trend 
0 @ Brooks2018 
Bear Trend From The Open was a 
Small PB Bear Trend 
Bear Trend From The Open refers to a bear 
trend that begins within the first 5 bars or 
so and lasts about 20 or more bars 
If only 10 bars, it is not a trend and 
another term would be better, 
like Wedge bottom or Sell Climax 
It then evolved into a bear channel

DB: Bull Trend From The Open 
I~ 
Bull Trend From The Open refers to a bull trend 
that begins within the first 5 bars or so 
and lasts about 20 or more bars 
If only 10 bars, it is not a trend and 
another term would be better, 
like Wedge top or Buy Climax 
' 
.. ·ioQ +¢ 
_r--T•··· Q 
• 
... <---- Tight TR so TR Open

B Climax: 3 Types Are Low 1, Low 2, and Low 3 
C) @ Brooks2018 
Low 1 (''B Climax'') 
• • • • • • ........ 
Big bull bar : 
or bars is 
Buy Climax 
Any Buy Climax 
can be after 
gap up or down 
Buy Climax that immediately 
reverses down is a Low 1 Top 
Just call it a ''Buy Climax'' bottom 
• • 
• 
Low 2 (Double Top) 
1 
+ 
Gap up so 
possible 
Buy Climax 
2 
Buy Climax that needs 2 attempts to 
reverse down is a Low 2 Top 
But if the tops are about the same 
level and at about 10 or more bars 
apart, call it a Double Top 
This is a Low 2 top 
Low 3 (Wedge Top) 
Buy Climax that reverses down after 
3 legs up is a Wedge top

If a PB in a bull tries to resume up twice, 
it is a High 2 Bull Flag

a 2nd reversal up is a double bottom 
A High 2 Bottom is just a small DB 
( usually less than 10 bars)

LH DT 
2 strong selloffs on open, 
or 2 strong reversals up 
Here, gap up then selloff

!60 I 
t• a 
If a rally tries to down reverse twice, 
it is a Low 2 Top 
If a 1st rally fails and bull trend resumes, 
a 2nd reversal is a double top 
A Low 2 top is just a small DT 
(usually less than 10 bars)

Part 7 (GU to GU M) 
Slide 129 

Low 1 (''B Climax'') 
• 
. • 
Buy Climax that immediately 
reverses down is a Low 1 Top 
Just call it a ''Buy Climax'' bottom 
• • 
• 
Low 2 (Double Top) 
1 
2 
+ 
Buy Climax that needs 2 attempts to 
reverse down is a Low 2 Top 
But if the tops are about the same 
level and at about 10 or more bars 
apart, call it a Double Top 
This is a Low 2 top 
Low 3 (Wedge Top) 
Buy Climax that reverses down after 
3 legs up is a Wedge top

High 1 (''Sell Climax'') 
Any Sell Climax 
can be after 
gap up or down 
Sell Climax that immediately 
reverses up is a High 1 bottom 
Just call it a ''Sell Climax'' bottom 
• • 
• 
High 2 (DB) 
~ 
1 
2 
Big bear 
v 
bar is 
Sell Climax 
Sell Climax that needs 2 attempts to 
reverse up is a High 2 bottom 
If the bottoms a re at a bout the 
same level and about 10 or more 
bars apart, call it a Double Bottom 
This is a High 2 bottom 
High 3 (Wedge Bottom) 
• • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • 
Sell Climax that reverses up after 
3 legs down is a Wedge bottom

High 2, but big gap up 
and close to perfect DB 
Call it a double bottom bull flag 
If a PB in a bull tries to resume up twice, 
it is a High 2 Bull Flag 
If there is a swing down after the 1st attempt up, 
a 2nd reversal up is a double bottom 
A High 2 Bottom is just a small DB 
( usually less than 10 bars) 
2

2nd L was much lower 
Call it High 2 bull flag

apart, call it a Double Top 
This is a Low 2 top

LH DT 
/ 
2 strong selloffs on open, 
or 2 strong reversals up 
Here, gap up then selloff 
Then strong 2nd leg up 
If a rally tries to reverse down twice, 
it is a Low 2 Top 
If a 1st rally fails and bull trend resumes, 
a 2nd reversal is a double top 
A Low 2 top is just a small DT 
(usually less than 10 bars)

. 
. 
S Climax: 3 Types Are High 1, High 2, and High 3 
~ ~ ) @rezanazri2012 
~ , . 
High 1 (''Sell Climax'') 
Any Sell Climax 
can be after 
gap up or down 
Sell Climax that immediately 
reverses up is a High 1 bottom 
Just call it a ''Sell Climax'' bottom 
t'm1 mr.nazari.ir 
High 2 (DB) 
1 
2 
Big bear 
bar is 
Sell Climax 
Sell Climax that needs 2 attempts to 
reverse up is a High 2 bottom 
If the bottoms are at about the 
same level and about 10 or more 
bars apart, call it a Double Bottom 
This is a High 2 bottom 
High 3 (Wedge Bottom) 
• • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • • 
Sell Climax that reverses up after 
3 legs down is a Wedge bottom 
@brooks2018 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

extreme S Climax: B above Big Bull Reversal Bar

Bear Trend From The Open refers to a bear 
trend that begins within the first 5 bars 
or so and lasts about 20 or more bars 
If only 10 bars, it is not a trend and 
another term would be better, 
like Wedge bottom or Sell Climax

Triangle means balanced bulls and bears 
Apex of Triangle is area of agreement 
It is therefore a magnet and often gets tested 
Usually does not lead to strong trend up or down 
Often becomes TR day 
Expect either TR day or 
reversal after 2 - 3 hours

. • • • • 
Big Gap up and 
Wedge bottom is sell climax 
Many bears take profits after sell climax and many bulls buy 
Bears wait to S again until after giving bulls 2 chances to create trend reversal 
Minimum goal after any major bottom is 2 legs sideways to up 
Usually get about half as many bars as there were in the wedge 
If reversal down after only 5 bars (not here), likely PB in 1st leg up, 
not end of 1st leg up

Parabolic wedge top 
3 legs (bull bars separated by pause bars) up in tight channel 
3 
S reversal down below bear bar closing near its L 
+ 
OT'++ -----
Q -----
In parabolic wedge top, 
3rd push up usually undershoots the line 
For undershoots, I draw the line connecting the 1st and 3rd points, 
and the 2nd is above the line (overshoot) 
Parabolic wedge tops are usually not as climactic as parabolic wedge 
bottoms where 3rd leg usually overshoots (breaks below) the channel

Creates High 2 Bull Flag

and there is a 2nd entry, 
the PB is a H2 bull flag

PB is High 1 buy signal setup 
(Hl bull flag) 
Every DB is H2 
H3 = triangle or wedge 
Hl, H2, and H3 are pullbacks in bull trend or TR 
Beginners should look for strong bull trend

no more than 1 close below EMA

above bull bar that closes near its high

It is therefore a High 1 bull flag buy signal bar

Part 9 (H to L) 
Slide 4 

In bull trend or TR, 
if H of bar is at or below H of prior bar, 
it is a High 1 B setup (bull flag) 
It is a PB or pause in bull trend 
If current bar trades above H of that bull flag bar, 
it triggers the High 1 buy signal 
5 consecutive High 1 bull flags 
Very strong bull trend 
9 
Unusual to have more than 
3 consecutive H 1 bull flags 
I+ 
A High 1 pullback in a bull trend is a bull flag 
It is therefore a High 1 bull flag buy signal bar 
because trends weaken over time

In bear trend or TR, 
if L of bar is at or above L of prior bar, 
it is a Low 1 S setup (bear flag) 
It is a PB or pause in bear trend 
If current bar trades below low of that bear 
flag bar, it triggers the Low 1 S signal 
5 consecutive Low 1 bear flags 
Very strong bear trend 
Unusual to have 
more than 3 consecutive Ll bear flags 
because trends weaken over time

1~Limit Down: Bull Trend Reversal 
.~@rezanazri2012 
Opened Limit Down after Globex session was Limit Down (''trading halt'') 
Trading usually resumes with a big gap down, like today 
Once resumes trading, there is typically a brief big selloff 
There is then sometimes a big reversal up, like today 
Here, big reversal up from S Clima~ ........ · ········ 
········· 
I 
·-
••• 
••• 
••• ••••• ••••• 
There was no Globex or Eminis back then, 
only S&PSOO futures 
The CME and NYSE can change the 
rules for halting trading at any time 
I remember they created the Limit Down 
rules after the 1987 Crash and have 
changed the rules many times 
in the years since 
They currently halt Globex trading after a 5% drop 
The current levels for stopping trading in the day session are 7%, 13%, and 20% 
Trading resumes when the NYSE cash equity market resumes trading

1~How to Draw Lines: When to Use Point 2 or 3 
.~@rezanazri2012

Most wedges do not have lines that perfectly touch all 3 points 
Does not matter if draw line using points 1 and 2 or 1 and 3 
Goal is to understand that market has tried 
a BO in one direction 3 times and is failing 
Odds favor an attempt in opposite direction 
•• 
(went beyond line) 
······• .... 1

3 is 
Undershoot 
(did not reach line) 
undershoot

overshoot 
When choosing between undershoot and overshoot,

■ Draw lines to highlight, and 
understand what market is doing 
■ Overshoot (going beyond line), and 
undershoots (not quite reaching 
line), are common 
■ Usually do not see them 
until pattern is complete 
[tg] mr.nazari.ir 
Overshoot 
Undershoot 
When there are 2 choices for a line, 
one with an undershoot and 
the other with an overshoot, 
choose the line with the overshoot 
It usually shows the shape better

H of bar is slightly above EMA (hard to see) 
But, bar never went above EMA 
EMA got pulled down to below top of bar once bar closed on its L 
EMA is calculated using the close of the bar 
A close near the L of bar pulled the average below the H 
This still qualifies as a touch of the EMA, 
even though the bar never rallied all the way up to the EMA

Why is there always a pullback 
to the EMA every day? 
Because once the market is below it 
for 60 bars or so, the stop is far above 
Institutions must reduce risk 
Easiest way is to take some profits 
That results in a PB to the EMA

[m] mr.nazari.ir 
EMA Gap Bars 
The 1st time the bears do anything in a bull trend, they usually fail 
They put 5 bars completely below the EMA (EMA Gap Bars} 
That usually becomes a failed reversal 
However, moving average gap bars typically lead to the 
final leg of the bull trend before the bears get a reasonable MTR 
A good MTR has a 40% chance of leading to a swing down

Micro Emini: Sometimes Different Price from Emini 
~ 
@rezanazri2012 
< 
Micro Emini 
Tail on top of Micro Emini 
is 6 ticks taller than Emini's tail 
Blue lines show that the 2 charts 
are otherwise almost identical 
Emini 
Price of Emini and Micro Emini 
rarely differ by more than 1 tick 
Robot computers Bone and S 
the other if they get even one 
tick out of line (arbitrage 
algorithms) 
Here, on quiet day, the Micro 
Emini's high was 6 ticks higher 
than the Emini's 
This is unusual 
~ @brooks2018

Micro Wedge: 3 Legs Within 4 - 7 Bars 
' • • ' 
2 3 
Micro wedge top is 3 - 5 legs up within 4 - 7 bars 
It is a wedge on a smaller time frame 
Micro wedge bottom is 3 - 5 legs down within 4 - 7 bars 
It · & W.~Qg~on a smaller time frame

1 
3 consecutive S climaxes (big bear bars followed by smaller bars) 
Call this a parabolic wedge bottom, and not a micro wedge, 
even though 3 pushes down in 6 bars 
Only use term ''micro'' when bars are relatively small 
3 
Smaller bear body so pause after BO bar 
This makes prior bar a leg in t e wedge

Slide 173 

Emini ltaily Chart 
All MTR bottoms 
are Double Bottoms 
Ra rely perfect 
1. Minor reversal breaks above bear trend line 
2. PB in bull trend reaches EMA and 
has about 10 or more bars (fewer if climactic rally) 
3. Good if EMA Gap bar since sign of strong bulls 
(able to put bar completely above EMA) 
4. Resumption of selloff that tests the prior low Q+o 
5. Resumption down has about 10 bars 
and is not very strong 
6. Good buy signal bar 
2nd bottom is 3 ticks higher 
7. 40% chance of swing up 
8. Expect at least 2 legs up

G] mr.nazar1.1r 
All MTR tops are Double Tops 
This one is perfect, which is rare 
1. Minor reversal breaks below bull trend line 
2. PB in bull trend reaches EMA and 
has about 10 or more bars (fewer if climactic rally) 
3. Good if EMA Gap bar since sign of strong bears 
(able to put bar completely below EMA) 
4. Resumption of rally that tests the prior high 
5. Resumption up has about 10 bars and is not very strong 
6. Good sell signal bar 
7. 40% chance of swing down (since TR and TR usually continues) 
8. Expect at least 2 legs down (here, came next day)

All MTRs are variations of double tops

60% of MTRs end up as minor and not major

current trend becomes an opposite trend

Today's reversal down had 2 legs and tested the L of the day, 
It was minor because it did not lead to a bear trend

G] mr.nazar1.1r 
HL MTR usually has a DB, and it usually is in a TR or Tight TR 
1st reversal up often is minor 
At this point, pattern either usually does not have enough bars 
or the sell off to H Lis too strong 
Bulls then need DB before major rally 
60% of MTRs end up as minor and not major 
A major trend reversal means the 
current trend becomes an opposite trend 
A minor reversal is a pullback or TR

MTR Bottom: LL MTR, then HL MTR 
~ 
@rezanazri2012 
• • • 
• 
• 
[m] mr.nazari.ir 
1. Minor reversal that breaks above bear trend line (often 2 - 3 choices) 
2. Rally reaches near EMA and has about 10 or more bars, 
or at least relatively strong bars 
{fewer need if Sell Clima~ 
t 
3. Good if EMA Gap bar since sign of strong bulls (not here 
{able to put bar completely above EMA) 
HL MTR 
(Not many bars 
so possibly minor) 
LL MTR 
(Not many bars so possibly minor) 
4. Resumption of bear trend that tests the bear low 
5. Selloff is at least 30% of rally, lasting about 10 bars 
(fewer if climactic or if big bars) 
6. Good buy signal bar 
7. 40% chance of swing up, having 2 or more legs 
8. Usually followed by HL MTR and 2nd leg up 
@@brooks2018

Nested HST: R Shoulder Is Smaller HST 
Here, the L shoulder and the head are also 
smaller Head and Shoulder Tops 
~ 
@rezanazri2012 
Big Head and Shoulders Top 
R shoulder is made of smaller HST 
so it is nested within big HST 
(''Nested HST'') 
However, ''nested'' requires only the R shoulder to be a HST

Open of day was L of day on this chart

overloading your computer and their systems 
Chart does not show every tick

m mr.nazari.ir 
• 
Open remained L for entire day 
On daily chart, open of day is L of day only about once a year 
99% of bars on daily chart have at least small tail above and below 
95% of the time, when the open of the 1st 5 min bar is the L, 
there is an early PB below the open 
If not, there is usually a later reversal to below the open

00 Bottom: Expanding Triangle on Smaller Time Frami
@,ezanazn2012 
5 min chart 
Consecutive 
utside .Bars 
1 min chart 
Think of every Expanding Triangle as 
an oo on some higher timeframe 
It is an oo spread over several bars 
It is a Breakout Mode pattern 
Consecutive outside bars (oo) means 
there are reversals above and below prior bar 
It is an Expanding Triangle on a smaller time frame, 
like here, on 1-minute chart 
Similar to how an ii is a Contracting Triangle on a smaller time frame 
Every 00 is always both a micro double top and double bottom

Outside Bar: Higher Probability if Actual Outside Bars 
~@,ezanazn2012 
3 bars with Higher Highs and Lows 
00 (consecutive outside bars) 
' BO Mode 
B above and 
S below 2nd 
outside bar 
3 bars with same highs and lows 
2nd and 3rd bars are 
b 
l~~g_and outside 
An outside bar is a bar with a high at or above the high of the prior 
bar and a low at or below the low of the prior bar 
It is a more reliable Breakout Mode pattern 
if the high is above the prior bar and the low is below 
This means 5;:!op orders trapped traders selling low and buying high 
An 00 is consecutive outside bars 
It is a higher probability pattern if the highs are above, 
and the lows are below the prior bar 
This is because a stop entry is triggered when the bar goes beyond 
the prior bar 
If there are consecutive bars that are fully outside, then there are at 
least 2 stop entries in opposite directions that failed 
That increases the chance that the next stop entry will succeed 
It is also higher probability when the body of the 2nd bar 
is in the direction of your trade 
That means buying above a bull bar or selling below a bear bar 
@@brooks2018 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Part 11 (N to P)

It is a Breakout Mode pattern 
that there are reversals above and below prior bar 
It is an Expanding Triangle on a smaller time frame, 
like here, on 1-minute chart 
Similar to how an ii is a 
Contracting Triangle on a smaller time frame 
Every 00 is always both micro double top and bottom 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Part 11 (N to P)

Outside Bar: Higher Probability if Actual Outside Bars 
~@,ezanazn2012 
3 bars with Higher Highs and Lows 
00 (consecutive outside bars) 
' BO Mode 
B above and 
S below 2nd 
outside bar 
3 bars with same highs and lows 
2nd and 3rd bars are 
b 
l~~g_and outside 
An outside bar is a bar with a high at or above the high of the prior 
bar and a low at or below the low of the prior bar 
It is a more reliable Breakout Mode pattern 
if the high is above the prior bar and the low is below 
This means stop orders trapped traders selling low and buying high 
An 00 is consecutive outside bars 
It is a higher probability pattern if the highs are above, 
and the lows are below the prior bar 
This is because a stop entry is triggered when the bar goes beyond 
the prior bar 
If there are consecutive bars that are fully outside, then there are at 
least 2 stop entries in opposite directions that failed 
That increases the chance that the next stop entry will succeed 
It is also higher probability when the body of the 2nd bar 
is in the direction of your trade 
That means buying above a bull bar or selling below a bear bar 
@@brooks2018 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Part 11 (N to P) 
Slide 195 
0

Most traders should only B options (puts, calls) or B spreads 
ATM put and call move half as much as underlying (e.g., SPY) 
If at least 1 month before expiration, 
at least 60% chance of at least some profit befor~ expiration 
If price of option doubles, take half or a 11 off 
If price of option loses 50%, exit with loss 
Only 20% of options double in value 
If option loses half of value, needs to double to get back to breakeven 
Since only 20% chance, better to exit and look for another trade 
ATM option has 60% chance of being profitable at some point 
ATM option has 50% chance of finishing ITM and 50% chance of being worthless (OTM) 
Needs to be ITM by at least size of purchase price to be profitable (but most exit before expiration) 
m mr.nazari.ir 
• 
@@brooks2018 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Part 11 (N to P) 
Slide 243 
0 
© 

Call Spread: Want Reward/Risk to Be about 3 to 1 
~ 
When buying spread, want price of spread to be about 1/3rd of the width of the spread 
Example, stock is 100 and you want to buy a call spread 
Choose an expiration that is 1- 3 months out 
B 1 ATM call 
Let's say you can buy it for 3.00 
S 1 higher call that would give you about 1.00 
Want the strike of the short call to be around resistance of the underlying 
m mr.nazari.ir 
• 
--
1 ~ 
@rezanazri2012 
@@brooks2018 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Part 11 (N to P) 
Slide 244 
0

Put Spread: Want Reward/Risk To Be About 3 to 1 
When buying spread, want price of spread to be about 1/3rd of the width of the spread 
Example, stock is 100 and you want to buy a put spread 
Choose an expiration that is 1- 3 months out 
B 1 ATM put 
~ 
Let's say you can buy it for 3.00 
S 1 lower put that would give you about 1.00 
Want the strike of the short put to be around support of the underlying

Orders for entry: Stop or limit orders? 
~ 
@rezanazri2012 
• • • ~-----
• • • • 
m mr.nazari.ir 
• 
Stop orders 
Consecutive trend bars 
Not much overlap 
Legs last more than 5 bars 
Limit orders 
Few consecutive trend bars 
Bad follow-through: alternating bull and bear bars 
A lot of overlap 
Big tails 
Legs last less than 5 bars

Small PB Bull Trend: TR More Likely 
Small PB Bull Trend is 
more likely bull leg in TR than strong trend 
Probability favors TR soon 
That means low probability of big trend 
Big trend then has good risk/reward 
Since pullbacks are small, risk is small 
That means reward is big

PB of 5o/o, 10o/o, or 20%: Can Be Support 
As a PB grows, traders expect it to reach magnets (support levels) 
PBs on daily chart often reach 2%, 5%, 10%, or 20%, once they get near, 
bears take profits and bulls buy so these levels are support 
Limit Down open 
Trading halted because 
market was 7% down, 
at circuit breaker 
·-
~ 
@rezanazri2012 
Reversed up exactly to 
the tick at a 20% PB 
> 
If the market falls below, many computers will get stopped out 
This increases the chance of at least some follow-through selling 
from the all-time high 
The bull computers buy just above and at these levels 
• • 
c;) mr.nazar1.1r 
• 
to try to prevent their stops from being hit

50% PB from Selloff: Sometimes Only Approximate 
~@,ezanazn2012 
•••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••• 
50% PB ······ i....++ 
•Qt .--
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••• 
When there is a pause or dip at a 50% PB, 
the market is usually either still in a bear trend or a TR 
Probability favors at least slightly lower 
If a person sold exactly at 50%, risk to a new high is exactly 
equal to a reward of a test of the low 
m mr.la~!ciJ?r.qbability is usually slightly better for the bears 
• 
50% pullback is the most important PB 
Sometimes it is exact, and 
other times it is approximate, like today 
Some traders place a limit order to 
sell exactly at 50% or 1 tick below 
Others sell on a reversal 
down from a 50% PB 
@@brooks2018 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com 
Part 13 (SN to SZ) 
Slide 297 
0 
© 

50% Pullback: Usually Approximate

50% PB 
---------------------------------
m mr.nazari.ir 
• 
1@rezanazn201.2 
50% pullback is the most important PB 
Sometimes it is exact, and 
other times it is approximate, like today 
Some traders place a limit order 
to buy exactly at 50% or 1 tick above 
Others buy on a reversal 
up from a 50% PB

50% Pullback: Usually Approximate 
~ 
@rezanazri2012 
Reversal down from 
50% pullback to 
yesterday's close 
---------- -------------------------
Breakout 
above 
50% PB 
•••• • 
Traders look for trend to resume at a 50% pullback, 
or for complete reversal of the trend 
50% is halfway between the top and bottom 
••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••• 
•••••••••••••• 
• ••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••• 
•••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••• 
Target is top or bottom of trend and stop is at other end 
so risk is same as reward 
If trend was strong and the 50% retracement was weak, 
the odds favor trend resumption 
•••••••••••••••• ••••••••• 
• ••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••• 
If the pullback was stronger than the trend, 
m mr.nazari.ir 
• 
the odds favor a complete reversal 
@@brooks2018

50% PB: After Reversal down from B Climax 
~ 
@rezanazri2012 
• • 
·············l.·················· 
o+9T --------~ _!+t:o~
_:----
50% pullback is the most important PB 
Sometimes it is exact, like today 
Some traders place a limit order 
to B exactly at 50% or 1 tick above 
Others Bon a reversal up from a 50% PB 
•••••••••••••••••••• 
When there is a pause or bounce at a 50% PB, 
the market is usually either still in a bull trend or a TR 
Probability favors at least slightly higher 
If a person bought exactly at 50%, risk to a new low is exactly 
equal to a reward of a test of the high 
But probability is usually slightly better for the bulls 
• 1m:naz-a.r1:1r·······································································································································

Stop Wide: Trade Small or Wait 
~ 
3 choices when stop is far: 
Trade small 
Put stop just below 60% 
instead of below bottom of rally 
Wait if cannot use appropriate stop 
or trade small enough 
If the stop you need requires 
risking more than 3% of your account 
DO NOT TAKE THE TRADE 
Wait for a trade where you can risk only 1- 3%

just below 50% PB

Buying Pressure: B Close of Every Big Bear Bar 
There were many big bear bars closing near their lows 
Traders bought each of those closes, except in late selloff, and made money 
When traders consistently make money 
betting against bear breakouts, it is a sign of strong bulls 
Each big bear bar was a bear trap, trapping bears into betting on a bear BO 
This is Buying Pressure and it increases the chance of higher prices 
~

use wide stops, and scale in

Reverse Position: From Short to Long 
~ 
@rezanazri2012 
Pause at MM up based on 
where traders sold and 
then reversed to long 
Bigger bear bar in -~ 
4-bar S Climax after 
big gap down 
Possible Exhaustive 
S Climax and profit 
taking rally soon 
(cg] mr.nazari.ir 
......... Q 
•••• 
·-···· 
Most traders do not like to reverse 
They prefer to exit and wait at least a bar or two before 
~ taking trade in opposite direction 
Need time to emotionally transition from 
one direction to the other 
Sometimes, if can anticipate a possible failed trade 
that might reverse, trader will reverse 
Here, selling below a bigger bar late in 
a S Climax is low probability 
Expect profit taking and rally soon 
from Exhaustive S Climax 
If trader sold below big bear bar, 
he can reverse to long above big bull bar 
Reversal will usually make at least enough profit to erase loss 
Therefore, often profit taking PB at MM up 
Here, there was a PB at the MM target from traders who 
took partial profits, but rally continued much highti) 
.. -, @brooks2018 
BrooksTradingCourse.com 
BrooksPriceAction.com

Reverse Position from Long to Short: Failed Wedge %QUQJJ112 
Reverse position from long to short 
Bulls exit below bear bar or Wedge 
Bears S below Wedge, below bear bar closing near its L, 
or below bear bars after bear BO 
Wedge Bottom 
after gap down, but failed 
Reversal will usually make at least what was lost on long 
Became huge Bear Trend From The Open 
Bars were huge 
But, were relatively small by end of 
day after crash down 3% 
Despite big loss on the 1st buy, 
• • • • • • 
♦ • • 
♦ • • 
♦ • 
if able to reverse below Wedge or go short below bear BO bar, 
you could have made many times the early loss 
Never give up because market rewards traders who are persist 
Always assume that any setup on the open has a 50% chance of being a trap 
Be prepared to do the opposite and expect to make back the loss or more 
(cg] mr.nazari.ir

Big Signal Bar: Big Risk so Need Very High Probability~®rezanazn2012 
Big buy signal bar closing on its H 
Wedge bottom so good context 
~ 
Big B signal bar and good context are not enough 
Only take the trade if willing to hold for swing up 
Bulls need occasional 5 - 10 point wins 
to offset the 5 point losses that come 30% of the time 
when a buy setup is strong 
Bulls have 70% chance of bulls making 1- 2 point scalp 
But bar is big and risk is 5 points 
Even winning 70% of the time will result in a net loss 
Today was one of those 30% days where the stop was hit 
Stop --

Rollover Day: Trade Expiring Futures Contract 
~ 
@rezanazri2012 
Rollover Day 
Today is rollover day in the Emini 
New front month, but expiring 
contract has much more volume 
The CME adjusted continuous 
futures contracts last night, 
and now based on the front month 
The volume shows that traders are 
sti 11 mostly trading the expiring 
contract today, which will continue 
to trade for another week 
Switch to trading front month on 
the day following rollover 
Does not matter much because the 
2 contracts are highly arbitraged 
Usually a few bars a day where 
the high or low of a bar, is above 
or below high or low of prior bar, 
on: 11iw

Wedge bottom 
The 3rd reversal up in a micro wedge is usually not the 3rd buy signal 
because the 1st reversal is not a reliable buy signal, and the 2nd 
often is not a credible B signal either 
~ herefore, the micro wedge B signal {3 rd reversal up) is either the 
2nd or 1st a ct u a I B sign a I 
Ihe 3 reversals are part of the same leg down and not 3 legs down 
It can be a wedge on a smaller time frame, but trade the chart in 
front of you 
3rd reversal up was only the 1st credible buy signal 
This is not a 3rd B signal within one pattern

Wedge Top: 3rd Top Is Usually Not 3rd S Signal 
(cg] mr.nazari.ir 
Wedge Top with 3 clear legs up, not micro wedge 
It was the 3rd reversal down 
but only the 1st credible sell signal 
t 
The 3rd reversal down in a Wedge is usually not the 
3rd sell signal because the 1st reversal is not 
a reliable sell signal, and the 2nd often is not 
a credible S signal either ---
Therefore, the Wedge S signal (3rd reversal down) 
is either the 2nd or 1st actual S signal 
~

Two weeks ago, GME was $17 and traded 6 million shares 
Yesterday's high was 27 times higher 
and the recent volume was 33 times greater 
Extreme short covering attributed to aggressive day traders 
Retail traders (day traders, like you and me) chatted with each other 
on Reddit.com and decided to buy GME stock and call options 
to force the hedge funds to buy back their shorts 
The big moves might have started with retail traders, but the volume 
and moves are so big that it had to be mostly institutional trading 
Big moves mean big opportunity so funds must be dominating the 
trading, as they always do 
Low 112.25 
77% below high of day

Exhaustive S Climax: Biggest Bear Bar Late in Bear 
~®,ezanazn2012 
Biggest bear bar late in bear trend 
is a Bear Surprise and 
usually Exhaustive S Climax 
Usually will attract profit takers 
If many bears cover shorts, bear trend will pause 
Expect at least 2 legs sideways to up 
If many bulls buy, will get bull trend reversal 
60% chance it is the end of the bear or 
one more brief leg down and then the end

• mr.nazar1.1r 
Major Surprise Bar or Bars 
Unusually big bear Surprise Bar or Bars 
About 5 times the size of average recent bars 
Surprise Bar is the opposite of 
a trap, which is a BO that fails 
I used to call this a 
Dominant Feature 
Huge bar often creates Surprise Bar 
on higher time frame so expect 
2nd leg down on higher time frame 
Often leads to lower prices for several days, 
not just minor reversal and 2 legs down on 5-min 
Bear trend or TR likely, not bull trend

~ 
@rezanazri2012 
Daily chart 
Big bear days on daily chart are 
usually big sell climax days on~ minute chart 
Usually no follow-through selling on next day 
Often get bull trend day

[8] mr.nazari.ir 
Q•~j 
t? 
Often, 50% of selling is caused by option-selling firms 
They sell puts to make money 
During bear days, puts rapidly increase in value 
Creates big losses for those firms 
They have to sell stocks and futures to hedge 
This makes the selloff worse than what 
the fundamentals would cause

Consecutive Sell Climaxes: Evolve into TR 
One or more big bear trend bars closing on low is a sell climax 
3 or more consecutive sell climaxes 
with only 1~ 5 bar pullbacks usually creates an exhausted trend 
Most climaxes lead to TRs 
Some lead to a reversal, or reversal after TR 
Often, no reversal until next day, like here
